channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Australia to hold general elections on May 3 with inflation and a housing shortage major issues
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australians will go to the polls on May 3 for general elections with high costs of living and a shortage of housing likely weighing against the government as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese 's center-left Labor Party seeks a second three-year term.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Eleven 'cranker colony' suspects charged after allegedly pilfering Florida home of deployed Air Force member
item.description: Florida authorities have arrested 11 people with drug and theft crimes after they allegedly broke into the Florida home of a deployed airman.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How this auto union leader's support for Trump's tariffs scrambled labor politics again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has once again scrambled the politics of organized labor and the working class with his planned tariffs on auto imports.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FAA acknowledges it must do better after deadly DC air collision
item.description: The head of the Federal Aviation Administration told Congress during a hearing Thursday about a midair collision over Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people that the agency must do more to ensure flying remains safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man accused of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas arrested, police say
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man who set fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas and who painted the word “resist” for authorities to find at the scene has been arrested.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Was classified information shared? Senators overseeing military request probe into Signal leak
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The top Republican and Democrat on the Senate Armed Services Committee requested an investigation Thursday into how Trump national security officials used the Signal app to discuss military strikes and a federal judge said he would order the preservation of the messages, ensuring a
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, AAMER MADHANI and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A string of crashes this year along with a near miss have brought attention to air travel, which remains overwhelmingly safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Civil rights groups say push to dismantle Education Department will undo hard-won gains
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rights of Americans to self-determine how to educate their children — a hotly contested matter that stretches back to at least the Civil War — have long been intertwined with the principle of equally and equitably educating children across racial lines.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Philadelphia detectives in perjury trial involving a 2016 murder exoneration
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The trial of three long-retired Philadelphia detectives accused of lying about evidence in a homicide case that later ended with an exoneration wrapped up with a mixed verdict Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Violin thief-turned-bank robber sentenced to 4 years in prison
item.description: A California man convicted of orchestrating a scheme to steal valuable violins, and then robbing a bank when he realized federal investigators were closing in on him, has been sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who have expressed their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY county clerk refuses to file Texas' fine for doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills
item.description: A county clerk in New York refused Thursday to file a more than $100,000 judgment from Texas against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills to a woman near Dallas, setting up a potential challenge to laws designed to shield abortion providers who serve patients in states with abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors in the killings of 4 Idaho students seek to use man's crime scene paper as evidence
item.description: Prosecutors in the case of a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students are seeking to use as evidence a college paper he wrote that shows his knowledge of crime scenes, according to a court filing this week Bryan Kohberger, 30, is accused in the stabbing deaths of Ethan Chapin
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tostitos chips sold in 13 states recalled over undeclared allergen
item.description: Frito-Lay issued the recall Wednesday, after it was discovered that some bags of Tostitos contained the wrong product.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court refuses to halt an order for the rehiring of thousands of fired federal workers
item.description: An appeals court in California has refused to halt a judge’s order requiring the Trump administration to rehire thousands of probationary workers who were let go in mass firings.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst And Janie Har, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration revokes state and local health funding
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services is canceling tens of billions of dollars in federal grants that state and local health departments were using to track infectious diseases, health disparities, vaccinations, mental health services, and other health issues.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas man flees scene after crashing car at 100 mph, leaving his critically injured family behind: police
item.description: Police are searching for a Texas man who crashed his car while driving at 100 mph and fled the scene leaving his critically injured family in the ditch, police say
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Kansas measles cases double to 23 and new Ohio outbreak sickens 10
item.description: A measles outbreak in Kansas doubled in less than a week to 23 cases and has "a possible link” to outbreaks in Texas and New Mexico that have sickened more than 370, the state health department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: NATO issues new statement on fate of 4 missing Fort Stewart soldiers
item.description: WIESBADEN, Germany (WSAV) — The NATO Acting spokesperson said that the Secretary General was referring to news articles and did not confirm the death of four U.S. Army 3rd Infantry Division soldiers reported missing in Lithuania Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Babysitter checking for monsters finds man hiding under child's bed
item.description: A 27-year-old man has been arrested after a Kansas babysitter found him hiding under a child's bed, police said. At around 10:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KTLA 5

item.title: Education Department restores IDR student loan applications
item.description: The applications were put on pause after a federal appeals court ruled the Biden administration’s Saving on Valuable Education (SAVE) income-drive repayment (IDR) plan was illegal.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KTLA 5

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order seeking to overhaul US elections, including requiring proof of citizenship
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a sweeping executive action to overhaul elections in the U.S., including requiring documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote in federal elections and demanding that all ballots be received by Election Day. The order says the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Columbia student sues Trump admin over efforts to deport her
item.description: Columbia University student Yunseo Chung is suing the Trump administration amid its efforts to deport the 21-year-old, who has been in the U.S. since she was 7 and has lawful permanent resident status.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9
